description: Comprehensive e-commerce analytics pipeline that reads order, customer, product, and web session data from CSV/Parquet files, performs complex transformations including currency normalization, customer segmentation, RFM analysis, and product performance calculations, then outputs customer 360-degree profiles, product performance metrics, and daily KPIs to multiple formats including Parquet files, CSV, and PostgreSQL database for business intelligence and reporting.

columns.name: total_amount
columns.description: Total order amount in original currency

columns.name: item_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for order line item

columns.name: line_total
columns.description: Total amount for this line item

columns.name: rate
columns.description: Exchange rate from source to target currency

columns.name: page_views
columns.description: Number of pages viewed in session

columns.name: events
columns.description: Number of events triggered in session

columns.name: total_orders
columns.description: Total number of orders placed by customer

columns.name: last_order_date
columns.description: Date of customer's most recent order

columns.name: lifetime_revenue
columns.description: Total revenue generated by customer in USD

columns.name: payment_methods
columns.description: Number of unique payment methods used

columns.name: high_value_orders
columns.description: Number of high-value orders (top 10% by amount)

columns.name: total_page_views
columns.description: Total page views across all sessions

columns.name: rfm_segment
columns.description: RFM-based customer segment (Champion, Loyal, At Risk, etc.)

columns.name: customer_tier
columns.description: Customer tier based on revenue and order count (Platinum, Gold, Silver, Bronze)

columns.name: months_active
columns.description: Number of months between first and last order

columns.name: nps_proxy
columns.description: NPS proxy based on RFM segment (Promoter, Passive, Detractor)

columns.name: unique_customers
columns.description: Number of unique customers who bought this product

columns.name: order_count
columns.description: Number of orders containing this product

columns.name: abc_class
columns.description: ABC classification based on cumulative revenue (A=top 80%, B=80-95%, C=95-100%)

columns.name: order_count
columns.description: Number of orders placed on this date

columns.name: unique_customers
columns.description: Number of unique customers who placed orders

columns.name: total_revenue
columns.description: Total revenue in USD for the date

columns.name: avg_order_value
columns.description: Average order value for the date

columns.name: total_discount
columns.description: Total discounts given on the date

columns.name: total_orders
columns.description: Total number of orders placed by customer

columns.name: last_order_date
columns.description: Date of customer's most recent order

columns.name: lifetime_revenue
columns.description: Total revenue generated by customer in USD

columns.name: payment_methods
columns.description: Number of unique payment methods used

columns.name: total_page_views

columns.description: RFM-based customer segment (Champion, Loyal, At Risk, etc.)

components.name: extract_orders
components.description: Reads orders CSV file with data type coercion and date parsing for order_date and ship_date columns, ensuring proper data types for downstream processing
components.sql_text: pd.read_csv with parse_dates=['order_date', 'ship_date'] and dtype specifications

components.name: extract_order_items
components.description: Reads order items CSV file containing line-level order details with proper data type casting
components.sql_text: pd.read_csv with dtype specifications for numeric columns

components.name: extract_customers
components.description: Reads customer master data from Parquet file containing customer demographics and signup information
components.sql_text: pd.read_parquet(DATA_DIR / 'customers.parquet')

components.name: extract_products
components.description: Reads product master data from Parquet file containing product details, categories, and cost information
components.sql_text: pd.read_parquet(DATA_DIR / 'products.parquet')

components.name: extract_exchange_rates
components.description: Reads daily exchange rates for currency conversion with date parsing
components.sql_text: pd.read_csv with parse_dates=['rate_date']

components.name: extract_web_sessions
components.description: Reads web session clickstream data with session timestamps and engagement metrics
components.sql_text: pd.read_csv with parse_dates=['session_start', 'session_end']

components.name: clean_orders
components.description: Filters out cancelled and fraudulent orders, normalizes channel names into Online/Offline groups, and flags high-value orders (top 10% by amount)
components.sql_text: df[~df['status'].isin(['cancelled', 'fraudulent'])]; channel_map = {'web': 'Online', 'mobile': 'Online', 'app': 'Online', 'store': 'Offline', 'phone': 'Offline', 'catalog': 'Offline'}; df['is_high_value'] = df['total_amount'] > df['total_amount'].quantile(0.9)

components.name: normalize_currency
components.description: Converts all monetary amounts to USD using latest exchange rates, calculates USD equivalents for total_amount, discount_amount, tax_amount, shipping_cost, and computes net_amount_usd
components.sql_text: latest_fx = fx_rates[fx_rates['to_currency'] == 'USD'].sort_values('rate_date').drop_duplicates(subset=['from_currency'], keep='last'); df['fx_rate'] = df['currency'].map(latest_fx); df[f'{col}_usd'] = df[col] * df['fx_rate']; df['net_amount_usd'] = df['total_amount_usd'] - df['discount_amount_usd'] + df['tax_amount_usd']

components.name: enrich_order_items
components.description: Joins order items with product details to add category, brand, and cost information, calculates margin (line_total - cost_price * quantity), margin percentage, and assigns margin bands (Negative, Low, Medium, High, Premium)
components.sql_text: items.merge(products[['product_id', 'product_name', 'category', 'sub_category', 'brand', 'cost_price', 'is_digital']], on='product_id', how='left'); df['margin'] = df['line_total'] - (df['cost_price'] * df['quantity']); df['margin_pct'] = (df['margin'] / df['line_total'].replace(0, np.nan) * 100).fillna(0); pd.cut(df['margin_pct'], bins=[-np.inf, 0, 15, 30, 50, np.inf], labels=['Negative', 'Low', 'Medium', 'High', 'Premium'])

components.name: build_customer_360_order_agg
components.description: Aggregates order data by customer to calculate transaction metrics: total orders, first/last order dates, lifetime revenue, average order value, discount/tax totals, online/offline order counts, payment method diversity, and high-value order count
components.sql_text: orders.groupby('customer_id').agg(total_orders=('order_id', 'nunique'), first_order_date=('order_date', 'min'), last_order_date=('order_date', 'max'), lifetime_revenue=('net_amount_usd', 'sum'), avg_order_value=('net_amount_usd', 'mean'), total_discount_usd=('discount_amount_usd', 'sum'), total_tax_usd=('tax_amount_usd', 'sum'), online_orders=('channel_group', lambda x: (x == 'Online').sum()), offline_orders=('channel_group', lambda x: (x == 'Offline').sum()), payment_methods=('payment_method', 'nunique'), high_value_orders=('is_high_value', 'sum'))

components.name: build_customer_360_top_category
components.description: Identifies each customer's top spending category by joining order items with orders, grouping by customer and category, summing spend, and keeping the highest spend category per customer
components.sql_text: cust_items = items_enriched.merge(orders[['order_id', 'customer_id']], on='order_id'); cust_items.groupby(['customer_id', 'category']).agg(cat_spend=('line_total', 'sum')).reset_index().sort_values(['customer_id', 'cat_spend'], ascending=[True, False]).drop_duplicates(subset=['customer_id'], keep='first')

components.name: build_customer_360_top_brand
components.description: Identifies each customer's top spending brand by grouping customer items by brand, summing spend, and keeping the highest spend brand per customer
components.sql_text: cust_items.groupby(['customer_id', 'brand']).agg(brand_spend=('line_total', 'sum')).reset_index().sort_values(['customer_id', 'brand_spend'], ascending=[True, False]).drop_duplicates(subset=['customer_id'], keep='first')

components.name: build_customer_360_distinct_products
components.description: Counts the number of distinct products purchased by each customer
components.sql_text: cust_items.groupby('customer_id')['product_id'].nunique().reset_index().rename(columns={'product_id': 'distinct_products'})

components.name: build_customer_360_web_agg
components.description: Aggregates web session data by customer to calculate engagement metrics: total sessions, page views, events, conversion count/rate, last session date, mobile session count, and days since last session
components.sql_text: web_sessions[web_sessions['customer_id'].notna()].groupby('customer_id').agg(total_sessions=('session_id', 'nunique'), total_page_views=('page_views', 'sum'), avg_page_views=('page_views', 'mean'), total_events=('events', 'sum'), conversion_count=('converted', 'sum'), last_session=('session_start', 'max'), mobile_sessions=('device_type', lambda x: (x == 'mobile').sum())); conversion_rate = conversion_count / total_sessions * 100

components.name: build_customer_360_rfm
components.description: Calculates RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) scores using quintile ranking: recency based on days since last order (5=most recent), frequency on total orders (5=most frequent), monetary on lifetime revenue (5=highest), and assigns RFM segments (Champion, Loyal, At Risk, Lost, etc.)
components.sql_text: rfm['recency_score'] = pd.qcut(rfm['days_since_last_order'], 5, labels=[5, 4, 3, 2, 1]).astype(int); rfm['frequency_score'] = pd.qcut(rfm['total_orders'].rank(method='first'), 5, labels=[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]).astype(int); rfm['monetary_score'] = pd.qcut(rfm['lifetime_revenue'].rank(method='first'), 5, labels=[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]).astype(int); rfm['rfm_total'] = rfm['recency_score'] + rfm['frequency_score'] + rfm['monetary_score']; assign_rfm_segment function with rules for Champion, Loyal, New Customer, At Risk, Lost, Big Spender, Regular

components.name: build_customer_360_tier_assignment
components.description: Assigns customer tiers based on lifetime revenue and order count: Platinum (≥$50K revenue AND ≥50 orders), Gold (≥$20K AND ≥20), Silver (≥$5K AND ≥5), Bronze (all others)
components.sql_text: def assign_tier(row): if row['lifetime_revenue'] >= 50000 and row['total_orders'] >= 50: return 'Platinum'; elif row['lifetime_revenue'] >= 20000 and row['total_orders'] >= 20: return 'Gold'; elif row['lifetime_revenue'] >= 5000 and row['total_orders'] >= 5: return 'Silver'; else: return 'Bronze'

components.name: build_customer_360_ltv_prediction
components.description: Calculates predicted 3-year lifetime value by computing monthly spend rate (lifetime_revenue / months_active) and projecting 36 months forward, setting to 0 for customers inactive >365 days
components.sql_text: order_agg['months_active'] = ((order_agg['last_order_date'] - order_agg['first_order_date']).dt.days / 30.44).clip(lower=1); order_agg['monthly_spend_rate'] = order_agg['lifetime_revenue'] / order_agg['months_active']; order_agg['predicted_3yr_ltv'] = np.where(order_agg['days_since_last_order'] > 365, 0, order_agg['monthly_spend_rate'] * 36)

components.name: build_customer_360_final_merge
components.description: Merges all customer analytics components (demographics, transaction aggregations, product preferences, web engagement, RFM scores) into comprehensive customer 360 view with derived fields like full_name and NPS proxy
components.sql_text: customers.merge(order_agg, on='customer_id', how='left').merge(top_category, on='customer_id', how='left').merge(top_brand, on='customer_id', how='left').merge(distinct_products, on='customer_id', how='left').merge(web_agg, on='customer_id', how='left').merge(rfm, on='customer_id', how='left'); c360['full_name'] = c360['first_name'] + ' ' + c360['last_name']; c360['nps_proxy'] = c360['rfm_segment'].map({'Champion': 'Promoter', 'Loyal': 'Promoter', 'Big Spender': 'Promoter', 'At Risk': 'Detractor', 'Lost': 'Detractor'}).fillna('Passive')

components.name: build_product_performance
components.description: Builds product-level performance metrics by joining enriched order items with orders, aggregating by product to calculate quantity sold, revenue, margin, customer count, order count, sales dates, channel distribution, margin percentage, revenue ranking, and ABC classification based on cumulative revenue contribution
components.sql_text: joined = items_enriched.merge(orders[['order_id', 'customer_id', 'order_date', 'channel_group']], on='order_id'); joined.groupby(['product_id', 'product_name', 'category', 'sub_category', 'brand']).agg(total_quantity=('quantity', 'sum'), total_revenue=('line_total', 'sum'), total_margin=('margin', 'sum'), avg_unit_price=('unit_price', 'mean'), unique_customers=('customer_id', 'nunique'), order_count=('order_id', 'nunique'), first_sale=('order_date', 'min'), last_sale=('order_date', 'max'), online_sales=('channel_group', lambda x: (x == 'Online').sum())); ABC classification: pd.cut(cumrev_pct, bins=[0, 80, 95, 100.01], labels=['A', 'B', 'C'])

components.name: build_daily_kpi
components.description: Builds daily KPI summary by grouping orders by date and calculating daily metrics: order count, unique customers, total revenue, average order value, total discounts, online orders, plus rolling 7-day and 30-day revenue/order totals and day-over-day revenue change percentage
components.sql_text: orders.groupby(orders['order_date'].dt.date).agg(order_count=('order_id', 'nunique'), unique_customers=('customer_id', 'nunique'), total_revenue=('net_amount_usd', 'sum'), avg_order_value=('net_amount_usd', 'mean'), total_discount=('discount_amount_usd', 'sum'), online_orders=('channel_group', lambda x: (x == 'Online').sum())); daily['rolling_7d_revenue'] = daily['total_revenue'].rolling(7, min_periods=1).sum(); daily['dod_revenue_change'] = daily['total_revenue'].pct_change() * 100

components.name: load_to_parquet_customer_360
components.description: Writes customer 360 DataFrame to Parquet file at output/customer_360.parquet using PyArrow engine
components.sql_text: df.to_parquet(OUTPUT_DIR / 'customer_360.parquet', index=False, engine='pyarrow')

components.name: load_to_parquet_product_performance
components.description: Writes product performance DataFrame to Parquet file at output/product_performance.parquet using PyArrow engine
components.sql_text: df.to_parquet(OUTPUT_DIR / 'product_performance.parquet', index=False, engine='pyarrow')

components.name: load_to_csv_daily_kpi
components.description: Writes daily KPI DataFrame to CSV file at output/daily_kpi.csv
components.sql_text: df.to_csv(OUTPUT_DIR / 'daily_kpi.csv', index=False)

components.name: load_to_postgres_customer_summary
components.description: Writes customer 360 DataFrame to PostgreSQL table public.customer_summary using SQLAlchemy engine with replace mode
components.sql_text: df.to_sql('customer_summary', engine, schema='public', if_exists='replace', index=False)

column_lineage.notes: Count of unique orders per customer, excluding cancelled and fraudulent orders
Total revenue generated by customer in USD after currency conversion

column_lineage.transformation: IF lifetime_revenue >= 50000 AND total_orders >= 50 THEN 'Platinum' ELIF lifetime_revenue >= 20000 AND total_orders >= 20 THEN 'Gold' ELIF lifetime_revenue >= 5000 AND total_orders >= 5 THEN 'Silver' ELSE 'Bronze'

intermediate_steps.expression: assign_tier function with revenue and order thresholds

column_lineage.notes: Customer tier based on lifetime revenue and order frequency thresholds

column_lineage.transformation: assign_rfm_segment based on recency_score, frequency_score, monetary_score quintiles

intermediate_steps.expression: pd.qcut for R/F/M scores + assign_rfm_segment function

column_lineage.notes: RFM-based customer segmentation (Champion, Loyal, At Risk, Lost, etc.)
Product category with highest customer spend
Percentage of web sessions that resulted in conversion
Predicted 3-year lifetime value based on monthly spend rate, set to 0 for inactive customers
Total revenue generated by each product
Total margin earned from each product

intermediate_steps.expression: cumulative['cumrev_pct'] = cumulative['total_revenue'].cumsum() / cumulative['total_revenue'].sum() * 100; pd.cut(cumulative['cumrev_pct'], bins=[0, 80, 95, 100.01], labels=['A', 'B', 'C'])

column_lineage.notes: ABC classification: A=top 80% revenue contributors, B=80-95%, C=95-100%
Same as customer_360.parquet - direct copy to PostgreSQL
